data.title: Reliance, Hero MotoCorp lead rise in India's stock benchmarks
data.description:
** India's equity benchmarks Nifty 50 and Sensex rise about 0.3% each, inching towards record high, led by Reliance and Hero MotoCorp on improving earnings outlook

** Twelve of the 16 major sectors advance; the broader mid-caps and small-caps rise about 0.4% each

** Index heavyweight Reliance Industries rises 1.5% to a 5-month high on upbeat views of UBS and Motilal Oswal on future earnings in oil-to-chemicals and new energy businesses, respectively

** Two-wheeler maker Hero MotoCorp gains 2.7% to 14-month high on rating upgrades by Macquarie and J.P.Morgan

** A combination of recovering profit in Indian corporates along with more reasonable valuations and improving flows could bring an end to market consolidation, boosting markets, says Rahul Singh, chief investment officer of equities at Tata Asset Management

** Among stocks, drug maker Biocon dips 4.2% after Citi double downgrades to "sell" from "buy", citing pricing pressures and intensifying competition in biosimilars

data.title: Orlen's third-quarter core profit rises on downstream recovery
data.description:
Polish state-controlled energy group Orlen reported a near 20% rise in its third-quarter core profit on Thursday, helped by a strong recovery in its downstream segment even as revenue fell due to lower oil prices.

The company's core profit adjusted for changes in value of its oil inventories, or EBITDA LIFO, was 7.17 billion zlotys ($1.95 billion) in the quarter, up from 5.99 billion zlotys in the same period in 2024.

The state-controlled group is in the middle of a strategic pivot after diversifying crude supplies away from Russia while revamping its petrochemical business. In October, it offered to buy out ailing Grupa Azoty's polyolefins unit in a deal valued at $278 million.

Orlen's downstream segment's EBITDA LIFO result swung to a profit of 757 million zlotys from a loss of 2.75 billion a year earlier, mainly due to smaller impairment charges on its assets, the quarterly earnings report showed.

The improvement was partially offset by a sharp decline in the Upstream & Supply segment, where core profit fell by nearly 3 billion zlotys year-on-year due to lower margins from gas sales.

Adjusted for write-downs, Orlen's core profit reached 8.9 billion zlotys in the quarter, in line with analysts' estimate in a company-compiled consensus.

($1 = 3.6690 zlotys)

data.title: Polish insurer PZU beats third-quarter expectations
data.description:
Poland's largest insurer PZU reported an 64% yearly rise in its third-quarter profit on Thursday, as higher revenues from premiums coupled with lower costs of insurance.

The company's net profit neared 2 billion zlotys ($545 million) in the quarter, exceeding the 1.61 billion zlotys analysts polled by the company had forecast.

($1 = 3.6684 zlotys)

data.title: South African Markets - Factors to watch on November 20
data.description:
The following scheduled economic events, company announcements, equity and currency market moves may affect South African markets on Thursday.

ECONOMIC EVENTS

South African Reserve Bank interest rate decision (ZAREPO=ECI)

COMPANIES

Mr Price Group posts interim results

SOUTH AFRICAN MARKETS

The South African rand was little moved on Wednesday after the release of slightly better-than-expected inflation data as traders looked ahead to the central bank's interest rate decision due on Thursday.

On the Johannesburg Stock Exchange, the Top-40 index was last up 1.2%.

GLOBAL MARKETS

A relief rally swept across Asian markets and lifted stocks in early trading on Thursday as investors cheered Nvidia's market-topping earnings, while the dollar ticked up as traders braced for the release of delayed jobs data.

WALL STREET

U.S. stocks ended a choppy session higher on Wednesday, bouncing off recent losses as technology stocks rose ahead of Nvidia's quarterly results.

GOLD

Gold prices edged lower on Thursday, weighed down by a stronger dollar and reduced expectations for a Federal Reserve rate cut in December, with investors eyeing a delayed U.S. jobs report.

EMERGING MARKETS

For the top emerging markets news, double click on

- - - -

Some of the main stories out in the South African press:

BUSINESS DAY

- U.S. warns SA against G20 declaration amid boycott

NEWS24

- Eskom overshot its own pollution target by 5 times
